title: rosa × collina var. fastigiata
intro: Rosa × collina var. fastigiata, also known as the fragrant rose or Rosa rugosa, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is a shrub native to East Asia and is known for its fragrant flowers, edible hips, and medicinal properties. The plant contains salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, relieving pain, and reducing fever. Herbal preparations of Rosa rugosa include tea, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
